name: Mnemosyne Memory
description: Local-first AI memory layer. Gives the agent persistent cross-session memory via the mnemosyne CLI — store, recall, consolidate, and auto-capture file edits.

tools.name: memory_remember
tools.description: Store a durable memory that persists across sessions. Use for: user preferences, project facts, environment details, tool quirks, stable conventions. Write declarative facts, not instructions. Returns the memory ID.

content.description: The memory content to store. Write as a declarative fact ('User prefers concise responses'), not an instruction ('Always respond concisely').

source.description: Source tag: preference, fact, insight, identity, task, etc.

importance.description: Importance 0.0-1.0. Higher surfaces more often. Default 0.5.

tools.name: memory_recall
tools.description: Search persistent memories by semantic similarity. Use before asking the user to repeat context — checks if a preference, fact, or past decision is already stored. Returns ranked matches.

query.description: Natural language query to search for.

tools.name: memory_stats
tools.description: Show Mnemosyne memory statistics: working count, episodic count, knowledge triples, and bank info.

tools.name: memory_sleep
tools.description: Run memory consolidation: compresses old working memories into episodic summaries. Call after long sessions or when memory feels stale.

tools.name: memory_forget
tools.description: Permanently delete a memory by ID. Use when a fact is stale, wrong, or no longer applies.

hooks.name: auto-capture-edits
hooks.description: After a file-editing tool runs, stores a memory of what changed so the agent recalls it in future sessions.

defaultPrompts: What do you remember about my preferences?
